role: Core implementation directory containing the main source code modules for the ArchSpine semantic mirror system.
responsibility: Collectively, all modules in this directory implement the full pipeline for semantic mirroring: from template definitions and language-aware AST parsing, through CLI interaction and configuration orchestration, to scanning, rule matching, LLM integration, service orchestration, task execution, type definitions, and utility infrastructure, enabling the system to mirror, analyze, and produce structured architectural knowledge from any codebase.

children.role: This directory contains the core template definitions and documentation standards for the ArchSpine mirror system.
AST parsing and language discovery layer for the ArchSpine mirror system.

This directory constitutes the command-line interface layer of the ArchSpine semantic mirror system, responsible for routing user commands, rendering help and banners, and managing initialization and repository artifact strategies.
Core L2 aggregation orchestration logic for the ArchSpine mirror system, responsible for coordinating data collection, validation, and state construction across multiple L1 sources.
Core engine and service modules for the ArchSpine mirror system, handling scanning, aggregation, rule matching, context resolution, and fix generation.

Infrastructure layer providing foundational services for the ArchSpine mirror system, including configuration, persistence, LLM integration, credential management, prompt generation, and system utilities.
Service orchestration and business logic layer for ArchSpine commands and workflows.
Pipeline task orchestration and execution layer for the ArchSpine mirror system.
Defines the core type definitions, data contracts, and configuration interfaces for the ArchSpine mirror system, including protocol types and view artifact schemas.
Provides foundational infrastructure and utility modules for file synchronization, path normalization, locking, and CLI presentation within the ArchSpine system.
